question: Which of the following statements are true regarding a singly linked list?

answers.text: Simple list is a linear structure.
The simple list is a dynamic structure in the sense that the nodes can be added and/or eliminated anytime without knowing their number a priori.
A simple list contains nodes where two different nodes are heterogeneous in terms of stored data.
Simple list is a structure whose elements are referenced in sequential access.
A simple list contains nodes where two different nodes are homogeneous regarding the stored data.
Simple list contains nodes, and each node stores multiple elements of the same type.

question: Which statement is true regarding the pointer variable p at the time of Heap memory deallocation by calling the function free(p)?

answers.text: It retains its content, respectively the Heap memory address obtained at the time of allocation.
The variable p is deallocated from the Stack segment where it was allocated at the time of its definition.
The data is copied into Stack memory where it will be accessed via the pointer variable p.
It is reinitialized with the null pointer.
It is rewritten with a different Heap memory address.
